items.title: Trump's signature to appear on US currency in a first for sitting president
items.summary: President Donald Trump's signature will soon appear on all new US paper currency, the first time in history that a sitting president has signed American money, the Treasury Department announced Thursday. The move is just the latest example of Trump pushing his name and likeness on US institutions.

items.title: Football: France edge past Brazil in World Cup dress rehearsal in Boston
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé and Hugo Ekitike both scored as France found their bearings in Boston with a 2-1 friendly win over Brazil on Thursday. Les Bleus will return to Massachusetts in three months, with the state capital set to serve as their World Cup base camp. The sports recap of the day with France 24 journalists and a summary of world sports news, both on and off the field.

items.title: 'They're putting up quite a fight': Hezbollah pushes back against Israel's advancing troops
items.summary: FRANCE 24 correspondent Renée Davis reports from Beirut, where Israel on Friday carried out fresh air strikes on the once densely packed southern suburbs. And in the country's south, Shiite group Hezbollah said its fighters continued to push back against Israel's ground offensive.

items.title: Middle East war live: Iran launches strikes on Israel and Gulf sites
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Friday they conducted missile and drone strikes the previous day against targets in Israel and US-linked military facilities across the Gulf. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is in France for a meeting of G7 foreign ministers as Washington faces mounting criticism for its spiralling war on Iran. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: US judge temporarily halts Trump-era sanctions on AI firm Anthropic
items.summary: A US federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked sanctions imposed by the Trump administration on AI company Anthropic, saying the measures likely violated the law. The ruling freezes a Pentagon designation of the firm as a national security risk, protecting it while the government considers an emergency appeal.

items.title: 'Justice necessary': Yazidi survivors deliver harrowing genocide testimony amid int'l indifference
items.summary: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Clémence Bectarte, a lawyer at the Paris Bar, specializing in international criminal law and international human rights law. She speaks from a position shaped her direct involvement,  working with survivors of the Yazidi genocide, whose experiences reveal both the depth of the brutal systematic violence and the enduring struggle for recognition and basic justice. The testimonies presented in this case are not only legal evidence, but acts of resistance against

items.title: Trump to order immediate pay for TSA as 41-day shutdown continues
items.summary: President Donald Trump said Thursday he would order the immediate payment of TSA agents as Congress struggles to end a 41-day funding shutdown that has snarled airports, left workers unpaid and forced officials to consider emergency measures, including shifting money or invoking a national emergency.

items.title: South Africa disinvited from G7 in France, backtracks on initial claim of US pressure
items.summary: South Africa announced Thursday that it has been excluded from the G7 summit in France this June, sparking tensions with the United States. Initially blaming Washington for pressure on Paris, Pretoria later backtracked, emphasising ongoing diplomatic engagement.

items.title: Transgender athletes barred from female category events at Olympics
items.summary: The International Olympic Committee said on Thursday it was re-introducing testing for gender to determine eligibility to compete in the female category, preventing transgender women from competing. The screening will mean Olympic women's sports from the 2028 Los Angeles Games will be limited to biological females, which would also rule out those with differences in sexual development (DSD). FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes reports.

items.title: Trump aura sa signature sur les billets de banque américains, une première pour un président
items.summary: La signature de Donald Trump va figurer sur les futurs billets de banque américains, a annoncé le Trésor jeudi. Depuis 1861, seule figurait, sur les billets verts, la signature du ministre américain des Finances et du trésorier des États-Unis.

translation.title: Trump will have his signature on US bank notes, a first for a president
translation.summary: Donald Trump's signature will appear on future US bank notes, announced the Treasury on Thursday. Since 1861, only the United States Minister of Finance and Treasurer had signed green notes.

items.title: Les Bleus prennent leurs marques à Boston en battant le Brésil
items.summary: L'Équipe de France s'est imposée 2-1 contre le Brésil, jeudi soir à Boston, grâce aux buts de Kylian Mbappé et Hugo Ekitike. À trois mois du début du tournoi, Les Bleus poursuivront leurs préparations pour le Mondial en affrontant la Colombie dimanche à Landover, dans le Maryland.

translation.title: Blues take their marks in Boston by beating Brazil
translation.summary: The French team won 2-1 against Brazil on Thursday night in Boston, thanks to the goals of Kylian Mbappé and Hugo Ekitike. Three months from the beginning of the tournament, Les Bleus will continue their preparations for the World Championship by facing Colombia on Sunday in Landover, Maryland.

items.title: Soupçonné d'un double féminicide, un ex-policier français placé en détention provisoire au Portugal
items.summary: Un ex-policier français, arrêté mardi au Portugal en compagnie de ses deux enfants après une cavale de plusieurs jours depuis l'Aveyron, a été placé en détention provisoire jeudi. Les corps de sa compagne et de son ex-conjointe ont été découverts enterrés à une centaine de kilomètres du lieu de son interpellation. Déchu de son droit de garde, il avait déjà été condamné pour non-représentation d'enfant et harcèlement sur son ex-compagne.

translation.title: Suspected of a double femicide, a former French police officer in pre-trial detention in Portugal
translation.summary: A former French policeman, arrested on Tuesday in Portugal with his two children after several days of cavalry from Aveyron, was placed in pre-trial detention on Thursday. The bodies of her partner and former spouse were found buried about 100 kilometres from the site of her arrest. Deprived of his custody, he had already been convicted of non-representation of children and harassment of his former companion.

items.title: Comment la “Compagnie”, réseau d’influence russe, propage sa propagande anti-Ukraine en Côte d’Ivoire
items.summary: Un réseau d’agents russes spécialisés dans les opérations de désinformation, la “Compagnie”, a lancé une vaste campagne de propagande contre l’ambassade d’Ukraine en Côte d’Ivoire. Grâce à une fuite de documents inédits reçus par le média panafricain The Continent, la rédaction des Observateurs a pu enquêter, avec un consortium de médias, sur les rouages de cette organisation.

translation.title: How the Russian-influenced company is spreading its anti-Ukraine propaganda in Ivory Coast
translation.summary: A network of Russian agents specializing in disinformation operations, the Company, has launched a large-scale propaganda campaign against the Ukrainian embassy in Côte d'Ivoire. Thanks to a leak of unpublished material received by the pan-African media The Continent, the Observers' editors, together with a media consortium, were able to investigate the organization's workings.

items.title: De 250 à 700 dollars l’article : comment la Russie a influencé les contenus de médias ouest-africains
items.summary: Une fuite de documents confidentiels d'un réseau d'agents russes sur le continent africain révèle la manière dont la Russie a réussi à diffuser des centaines d'articles dans 35 médias francophones ouest-africains, parfois à leur insu. Avec un consortium international de médias coordonné par Forbidden Stories, la rédaction des Observateurs de France 24 a analysé ces documents stratégiques, qui montrent en miroir la porosité de ces médias face aux ingérences extérieures.

translation.title: From 250 to 700 dollars a article: how Russia influenced West African media content
translation.summary: A leak of confidential material from a network of Russian agents on the African continent reveals how Russia has managed to broadcast hundreds of articles in 35 West African French-speaking media, sometimes without their knowledge. With an international media consortium coordinated by Forbidden Stories, the editorial staff of the Observateurs de France 24 analysed these strategic documents, which reflect the porosity of these media in the face of external interference.

items.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient et indice "Taco" : le monde de la finance face à l'énigme Trump
items.summary: Des analystes financiers ont établi un indice “Taco”, censé modéliser les périodes lors desquelles Donald Trump change de politique. Une tentative d’introduire un peu de rationalité financière dans le comportement souvent jugé imprévisible du président américain. Surtout dans une période de conflit où chaque décision du locataire de la Maison Blanche a un impact économique et financier.

translation.title: War in the Middle East and "Taco" index: the world of finance facing the Trump puzzle
translation.summary: Financial analysts have set up a "Taco" index, supposed to model the periods during which Donald Trump changes politics. An attempt to introduce a bit of financial rationality into the behaviour often considered unpredictable of the US President. Especially in a period of conflict where every decision of the White House tenant has an economic and financial impact.

items.title: En direct : les Gardiens de la révolution visent des cibles en Israël et dans le Golfe
items.summary: Les Gardiens de la révolution iraniens ont dit vendredi avoir envoyé missiles et drones sur des cibles militaires et énergétiques en Israël et dans les pays du Golfe. Plus tôt, l'armée israélienne avait annoncé avoir mené des frappes d'ampleur sur Téhéran. Le chef américain de la diplomatie Marco Rubio est arrivé en France vendredi pour le second jour d'une réunion du G7. Il devrait pousser ses homologues à aider à la réouverture du détroit d'Ormuz. Suivez notre direct.

translation.title: Live: Revolutionary Guardians Target Targets in Israel and the Gulf
translation.summary: Iranian Revolutionary Guards said on Friday they sent missiles and drones to military and energy targets in Israel and the Gulf countries. Earlier, the Israeli army announced that it had carried out extensive strikes on Tehran. The American Chief of Diplomacy Marco Rubio arrived in France on Friday for the second day of a G7 meeting. It should urge its counterparts to help reopen the Strait of Ormuz. Follow our direct.

items.title: IA : une juge suspend les sanctions de l'administration Trump contre Anthropic
items.summary: Une juge fédérale américaine a estimé jeudi que les sanctions de l'administration Trump à l'encontre de l'entreprise Anthropic violaient probablement la liberté d'expression. Ces sanctions sont donc suspendues pour sept jours. Anthropic avait publiquement refusé que son IA Claude soit utilisée pour la surveillance de masse des citoyens américains ou pour opérer des armes autonomes.

translation.title: IA: A judge suspends Trump administration sanctions against Anthropic
translation.summary: A US federal judge on Thursday found that Trump's sanctions against Anthropic probably violated freedom of expression. The sanctions are therefore suspended for seven days. Anthropic had publicly refused that his AI Claude should be used for mass surveillance of American citizens or to operate autonomous weapons.

items.title: Files d'attente dans les aéroports : les agents de sécurité vont être enfin payés, dit Donald Trump
items.summary: Alors qu'une paralysie budgétaire partielle bloque depuis plusieurs semaines le versement des salaires des agents en charge des contrôles de sécurité dans les aéroports américains, théâtres de files d'attente interminables, le président américain Donald Trump a affirmé jeudi avoir ordonné au ministère de la Sécurité intérieure de payer les agents, sans préciser comment.

translation.title: Airport queues: security officers will finally be paid, says Donald Trump
translation.summary: While a partial budget paralysis has been blocking the payment of salaries for security screening officers at U.S. airports for several weeks, the US President Donald Trump said on Thursday he ordered the Ministry of Homeland Security to pay the officers, without specifying how.

items.title: L'Afrique du Sud, d'abord invitée, a été écartée du G7 prévu en juin en France
items.summary: La France a retiré à l'Afrique du Sud son invitation au G7 de juin prochain, qui doit se tenir à Évian. Pretoria a d'abord dénoncé jeudi des pressions américaines pour expliquer cette mise à l'écart, avant que le président sud-africain lui-même fasse marche arrière. Washington, de son côté, nie toute implication. Paris assure n'avoir "cédé à aucune pression", mais avoir fait le choix d'un "G7 resserré".

translation.title: South Africa, first invited, was removed from the G7 planned in June in France
translation.summary: France has withdrawn its invitation to South Africa to the G7 next June, to be held in Evian. Pretoria first denounced on Thursday US pressure to explain this lay-off, before the South African president himself turned back. Washington, for its part, denies any involvement. Paris asserts that it has not given way to any pressure, but that it has chosen a tight G7.

items.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. Twenty-two years ago, with the deployment of Xi Jinping, then Secretary-General of Zhejiang Province, the “millions of projects” focusing on the environmental rehabilitation of rural settlements were launched. At that time, Comrade Xi had been the secretary of the Zhejiang province for more than six months and had been working for a long time. Why does he focus on the rehabilitation of the rural habitat environment? At the grass-roots level, Comrade Xi found that while the Zhejiang economy was growing rapidly, the rural areas were faced with “growth problems” and the villages were less than satisfactory. Out of 34,000 villages in the province, only 4,000 have a better habitat. It was described as “a village after a village, which is full of garbage”. And the farmers described it as: “Getting up early and making money, counting it in the trash, spending it in the hospital.” It can be said that the remediation of the habitat environment was an urgent aspiration of the rural population of Zhejiang at the time. The fact that millions of projects focus on small problems such as what villages to follow and how to deal with animal and animal dung is a matter of life for the farmers. The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.

items.title: Meduza’s readers in Russia on life without Internet
items.summary: Last week, Meduza asked its readers in Russia how they’re living amid mobile Internet shutdowns and restrictions, which have become worse in recent weeks. We received hundreds of responses from dozens of cities. Together, they show how much everyday life has changed — and how much harder basic tasks have become. We’re sharing some of the most notable responses, translated into English.

items.title: The Kremlin appears to be blocking Telegram. Meduza asked its Russia-based readers how well the crackdown is working.
items.summary: Late last week, many Telegram users in Russia began reporting serious disruptions, according to outage trackers Downdetector and Sboy.rf. Reports that Russian authorities planned to permanently block the popular messaging app in early April had been circulating for some time. But according to industry experts, the process may have begun two weeks ahead of schedule.

items.title: A longtime pro-Putin blogger published a tirade against the Russian president. Now he’s in a psychiatric hospital.
items.summary: Ilya Remeslo, a longtime pro-Kremlin blogger known for filing complaints against critics of the Russian authorities, made waves in the country this week when he published a Telegram post titled “Five reasons why I stopped supporting Vladimir Putin.” Days later, he was reported to have been hospitalized at a St. Petersburg psychiatric facility. Here’s what we know about Remeslo’s about-face.

items.title: Petrol, diesel price today: After excise duty cuts, will fuel rates in your city come down?
items.summary: The government has announced a reduction in excise duty on petrol and diesel. While excise duty on petrol has been cut, the levy on diesel has been removed altogether with an aim to cushion consumers from the surge in global crude oil prices.

items.title: PM Modi prayers to Lord Ram, observes Surya Tilak ceremony at Ayodhya's Ram Mandir virtually
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi offered prayers and joined the Surya Tilak ceremony at Ayodhya's Ram Mandir virtually. He conveyed greetings for Ram Navami, wishing for renewed energy and enthusiasm across the nation. Devotees gathered at temples nationwide, with Ayodhya seeing large crowds. Security was enhanced to manage the pilgrims. Ram Navami celebrates the birth of Lord Rama.

items.title: Watch: Massive ruckus in J&K assembly as NC MLAs protest over killing of Iran's Ali Khamenei
items.summary: National Conference MLAs protested the killing of former Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei and US-Israel military actions in Iran within the Jammu and Kashmir Assembly. The protest led to a scuffle with BJP MLAs, prompting an adjournment. Chief Minister Omar Abdullah condemned the conflict, stating regime change cannot be achieved through military bombardment.

items.title: Vaibhav Sooryanshi turns 15, now officially eligible, is India senior debut next?
items.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, turning 15 today, is now eligible for senior international cricket after a sensational IPL debut. His record-breaking performances, including a 35-ball century and U19 World Cup heroics, have positioned him as a top prospect.  Selectors are watching closely as he could soon represent India's senior men's team.

items.title: How one question from Google co-founder 'convinced' Demis Hassbis to sell DeepMind
items.summary: A casual walk at Elon Musk's birthday party in 2013 led to a pivotal question from Google co-founder Larry Page to DeepMind's Demis Hassabis. Page's offer of Google's vast resources to pursue artificial general intelligence convinced Hassabis to abandon his independent venture, ultimately leading to Google's acquisition of DeepMind in 2014.

items.title: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' BO day 9: Ranveer Singh starrer inches closer to netting Rs. 700 crore
items.summary: Aditya Dhar’s espionage epic, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', shows no signs of slowing down as it enters its high-stakes second weekend. Starring Ranveer Singh as the covert spy Hamza, the film has officially joined the elite Rs. 1,000 crore global club in just nine days, matching the record-shattering pace of Allu Arjun starrer 'Pushpa 2.' The sequel is now poised to surpass the Rs. 894.49 crore lifetime record set by the original 2025 released 'Dhurandhar.'

items.title: Centre announces extra 20% LPG allocation to states amid global energy crisis
items.summary: Amidst global energy supply concerns due to Middle East conflict, India has boosted commercial LPG allocation to 70% for industries like steel, auto, and textiles. This move aims to support labour-intensive sectors and those with specialized heating needs. The government also assured fuel security, refuting shortage claims and increasing refinery output.

items.title: Get rid of old assumptions: Microsoft HR makes it clear to employees that it's time to change
items.summary: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman has announced a significant restructuring of the HR division, prioritizing adaptability and speed to align with the company's AI-first strategy. This overhaul includes leadership changes and the formation of new teams focused on accelerating HR operations, shaping culture, and fostering workforce acceleration.

items.title: 'Historic first': Nepal gets a rapper, Gen Z PM as Balendra Shah takes oath
items.summary: Nepal has a new Prime Minister, Balendra Shah, the nation's youngest at 35.  A rapper-turned-politician and former Kathmandu mayor, Shah assumes office as the Rastriya Swatantra Party leader.  His rise signifies a shift, with his confrontational style against the federal government gaining traction.  This appointment follows the RSP's strong performance in recent elections.

items.title: Virat Kohli’s new lotus tattoo goes viral, artist reveals deeper meaning - watch
items.summary: Virat Kohli has unveiled a striking new full-sleeve tattoo ahead of IPL 2026, featuring a lotus and peony. This evolving artwork, created by Aliens Tattoo India, symbolizes his personal transformation and journey, with the lotus representing purity and resilience. The design integrates and refines his existing tattoos into a cohesive narrative.
